Title: An alternative pathway for the effective production of the omega-3 long-chain polyunsaturates EPA and ETA in transgenic oilseeds

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: omega-3/omega-6 long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis, alternative Δ9-pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates C18 polyunsaturated fatty acids linoleic acid and α-linolenic acid to the corresponding C20 products 20:2n-6 and 20:3n-3.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediates/precursors for ETA and EPA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: nutritionally valuable omega-3 fish-oil-like fatty acids; ETA is noted to have anti-inflammatory activity similar to EPA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed oil triacylglycerols; phospholipids; glycolipids of transgenic Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of C20 intermediates and contributes to EPA and ETA production in transgenic seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In Arabidopsis AP2/AP3, EhElo9 expression caused clear accumulation of 20:2n-6 and 20:3n-3, showing strong Δ9-elongase activity in seeds. 2. In the optimized AP5 pathway in Camelina, together with IsElo9 it supported production of ETA and EPA in seed oils. 3. The data imply preference for omega-3 substrate flow, though this was not formally proven because Pix3 could confound interpretation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in developing/mature seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed-specific expression under controlled growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic seed-specific expression in Arabidopsis and Camelina; GC-FID/GC-MS fatty acid profiling; lipidomics; acyl-CoA profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with reduced ALA and 20:1n-9 pools in AP5 context and altered endogenous seed-oil composition; no visible phenotypic perturbation reported.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful component for engineering plant seed oils to produce fish-oil-like omega-3 LC-PUFAs, especially when stacked with efficient downstream desaturases.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: lipidomics by LC-MS/MS and ESI-MS/MS; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as one of two Δ9 elongases in AP5. Apparent omega-3 preference is inferred, not directly demonstrated.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: omega-3/omega-6 long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis, alternative Δ9-pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Expected to desaturate 20:2n-6 and 20:3n-3 at the Δ8 position to generate DGLA and ETA, respectively.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursors/intermediates toward EPA and ETA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Did not support detectable downstream Δ8-desaturation products or EPA accumulation in transgenic Arabidopsis in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcΔ8 replaced EhΔ8 in AP3. 2. Although transcripts were detected in Arabidopsis, no Δ8-desaturation products nor EPA were observed. 3. Thus AcΔ8 appeared inactive in Arabidopsis seeds under the conditions used.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in Arabidopsis seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed-specific expression under controlled growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic expression in Arabidopsis; GC-MS/GC-FID seed fatty acid profiling; transcript PCR
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No beneficial EPA production detected; ineffective step in Arabidopsis background.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Poor candidate for engineering this pathway in Arabidopsis/Camelina based on this study.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Active previously in yeast, but not functionally evident in Arabidopsis seeds here.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: omega-3/omega-6 long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis, alternative Δ9-pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Expected to desaturate 20:2n-6 and 20:3n-3 at the Δ8 position to produce DGLA and ETA.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursors/intermediates toward EPA and ETA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Did not produce detectable Δ8-desaturation products or EPA in transgenic Arabidopsis in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In AP2, EhΔ8 transcripts were present in Arabidopsis. 2. No Δ8-desaturation products or EPA were detected, indicating lack of functional activity in this host context. 3. This step limited AP2 performance and motivated redesign of the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in Arabidopsis seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed-specific expression under controlled growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic expression in Arabidopsis; GC-MS/GC-FID seed fatty acid profiling; transcript PCR
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ineffective in Arabidopsis seeds; no target EPA accumulation from AP2.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not favored for further engineering in this platform based on poor in planta activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously active in yeast but not functionally evident in Arabidopsis seeds here.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: omega-3/omega-6 long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis, alternative Δ9-pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: efficient step; not the main bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates 20:2n-6 and 20:3n-3 to produce 20:3n-6 (DGLA) and 20:4n-3 (ETA); also shows off-target desaturation of 20:1n-9 to 20:2n-9.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms ETA and forms immediate precursor for EPA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: nutritionally valuable omega-3 fatty acids; ETA additionally noted as anti-inflammatory
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Camelina seed TAG; phospholipids, especially PE and PC for ETA; glycolipids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes ETA accumulation and supports EPA production, but also generates off-target 20:2n-9 and contributes to reduction of 20:1n-9.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsD8 was the functionally effective Δ8-desaturase in AP4/AP5, unlike EhΔ8 or AcΔ8 in Arabidopsis. 2. Its activity enabled accumulation of ETA and EPA in Arabidopsis and Camelina seeds. 3. Lipid profiling indicated PsD8 acts on phospholipid-linked substrates, enriching ETA in phospholipids. 4. Off-target desaturation of 20:1n-9 generated 20:2n-9 and lowered endogenous 20:1n-9.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in developing and mature Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed-specific expression under controlled growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic expression in Arabidopsis and Camelina; GC-FID/GC-MS fatty acid profiling; lipid class analysis; stereospecific lipid analysis; acyl-CoA profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Off-target production of 20:2n-9 and depletion of 20:1n-9; contributes to substrate dichotomy with acyl-CoA-dependent EhD5.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key enzyme for alternative Δ9-pathway engineering, but should ideally be paired with downstream enzymes matching phospholipid substrate pools to reduce bottlenecks and side products.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: lipidomics by LC-MS/MS and ESI-MS/MS; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper infers phospholipid dependence for PsD8 from lipid distribution data; its strong activity made it the preferred Δ8-desaturase in AP5.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: omega-3/omega-6 long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis, alternative Δ9-pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: appears bottlenecked in this pathway configuration due to substrate dichotomy; acyl-exchange into acyl-CoA likely rate-limiting for its action
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates DGLA and ETA at the Δ5 position to generate ARA and EPA, respectively, with preference for acyl-CoA substrates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target end product EPA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: nutritionally valuable omega-3 fish-oil-like fatty acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly enables EPA synthesis from ETA, but incomplete conversion causes substantial ETA accumulation due to substrate dichotomy.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EhD5 is the terminal desaturase producing EPA from ETA in the engineered alternative pathway. 2. The enzyme is believed to prefer acyl-CoA substrates. 3. In AP5 Camelina, ETA accumulated more strongly in phospholipids than EPA, indicating that acyl-exchange from phospholipids to acyl-CoA limited EhD5-dependent conversion. 4. Thus the pathway recreated a substrate-dichotomy bottleneck, making EPA production less efficient than ETA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in developing and mature Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed-specific expression under controlled growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic expression in Arabidopsis and Camelina; GC-FID/GC-MS fatty acid profiling; lipidomics; stereospecific TAG and PC analysis; acyl-CoA profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CnI/conlinin1 promoter in AP2/AP3 and AP5, seed-specific
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Incomplete ETA-to-EPA conversion; recreates substrate dichotomy bottleneck when paired with phospholipid-dependent PsD8; lowers engineering efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for EPA production but should be replaced or combined with a phospholipid-matched Δ5-desaturase, or with improved acyl-exchange modules, to raise EPA and lower ETA bottleneck.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: lipidomics by LC-MS/MS and ESI-MS/MS; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly discusses EhD5 substrate preference as a major mechanistic explanation for the observed lipid distributions.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: C18 Δ9 polyunsaturated fatty acid-specific elongase
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: omega-3/omega-6 long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis, alternative Δ9-pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates C18 polyunsaturated fatty acids such as LA and ALA to C20 products 20:2n-6 and 20:3n-3.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediates/precursors for ETA and EPA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: nutritionally valuable omega-3 fish-oil-like fatty acids; ETA also highlighted as anti-inflammatory
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seed oil triacylglycerols; phospholipids; glycolipids of transgenic Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to efficient entry of endogenous C18 PUFA substrates into the engineered pathway and supports ETA/EPA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AP4 using IsElo9 together with PsD8 and EhD5 yielded detectable EPA and ETA in Arabidopsis. 2. Inclusion of IsElo9 in AP5 improved pathway performance and enabled higher EPA/ETA accumulation in Camelina. 3. Data imply elongase-mediated flux may favor omega-3 substrates, though not formally proven.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in developing/mature Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed-specific expression under controlled growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic expression in Arabidopsis and Camelina; seed fatty acid profiling; lipidomics
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated within AP5 context with reduced ALA and altered endogenous oil composition, though no overt plant phenotype observed.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful elongase for building alternative Δ9-pathway constructs aimed at EPA/ETA accumulation in oilseeds.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: lipidomics by LC-MS/MS and ESI-MS/MS; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text also abbreviates this elongase as IsoELO9 in discussion; vector section uses IsElo9.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: omega-3/omega-6 long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis, precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts oleic acid to linoleic acid to increase availability of LA precursor for downstream LC-PUFA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for ETA/EPA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: nutritionally valuable omega-3 fish-oil-like fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports precursor formation for the engineered pathway but alone does not strongly depress oleic acid in the AP5 configuration.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsD12 was included to enhance LA levels and optimize EPA synthesis. 2. In the alternative AP5 pathway, its presence did not cause the strong oleic acid depletion seen with the conventional pathway containing O. tauri Δ6-desaturase. 3. The paper concludes PsD12 itself is not the main cause of oleic acid loss; pathway context matters.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in Arabidopsis and Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed-specific expression under controlled growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic expression; GC-FID/GC-MS seed fatty acid profiling; comparative pathway analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can contribute to changes in oleic and linoleic acid pools depending on pathway context, although no major visible phenotype was observed.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply enzyme, but should be evaluated in combination with downstream enzymes to avoid unwanted depletion of native oil components.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as supportive pathway enzyme rather than the main determinant of EPA accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: omega-3/omega-6 long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis, omega-3 conversion module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts C20 omega-6 fatty acids, notably arachidonic acid, to the corresponding omega-3 product EPA; more broadly increases omega-3 over omega-6 LC-PUFAs.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product and shifts flux toward omega-3 LC-PUFAs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: nutritionally valuable omega-3 fish-oil-like fatty acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of omega-6 C20 products to omega-3 forms, contributing to higher omega-3 than omega-6 LC-PUFA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pix3 was included to increase conversion of ARA to EPA. 2. In AP5 Camelina and Arabidopsis, omega-3 products accumulated to higher levels than the analogous omega-6 forms. 3. The authors note that Pix3 can confound direct assessment of elongase substrate preference because it converts omega-6 products into omega-3 forms.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: seed-specific transgene expression in Arabidopsis and Camelina seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed-specific expression under controlled growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic expression; GC-FID/GC-MS seed fatty acid profiling; comparative pathway analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May complicate mechanistic interpretation of upstream substrate preferences; alters balance of omega-6/omega-3 pools.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory enzyme to bias engineered seed oils toward omega-3 LC-PUFAs and reduce residual omega-6 intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a supportive conversion enzyme in AP2-AP5 rather than as the primary step creating the initial C20 backbone.
